abseqr 0.1.0
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • Analysing Nanobody CDR3 Sequences with abseqr

Plot CDR3 length distribution

Source: R/cdr3_length_dist.R
cdr3_length_dist.Rd

Produces a bar chart showing the frequency of each CDR3 length in the dataset. Sequences where CDR3 could not be extracted are excluded with a message. Use extract_cdr3() to add CDR3 data first.

cdr3_length_dist(
  data,
  fill = "#4C8CB5",
  title = "CDR3 Length Distribution",
  subtitle = NULL
)

Arguments

data

A tibble containing a cdr3_length column, as returned by extract_cdr3().

fill

Character. Bar fill colour. Default "#4C8CB5".

title

Character. Plot title. Default "CDR3 Length Distribution".

subtitle

Character or NULL. Optional subtitle.

Value

A ggplot object.

Examples

fasta_file <- system.file("extdata", "example_nanobodies.fasta",
                           package = "abseqr")
seqs <- read_fasta(fasta_file) |> extract_cdr3()
cdr3_length_dist(seqs)
